svg.text: DUTCH + POLISH — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 49D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— DUTCH — PASSAGE 1 · SPINOZA —
"De vrijheid is de erkenning van de noodzakelijkheid."
"Freedom is the recognition of necessity." — Baruch Spinoza (Amsterdam, 1677).
— DUTCH — PASSAGE 2 · PROVERB —
"Oost west, thuis best."
East west, home best.
"East or west, home is best." — Universal human longing; Dutch phrase ~16th century.
— DUTCH — BRIDGE PHRASE · REMEMBER FORWARD —
Dit werd voor jou gemaakt, gratis, door mensen die vooruit hebben gedacht.
This was-made for you, freely, by people who forward have thought.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who thought forward."
— LANGUAGE NOTE — "FORWARD" IN DUTCH —
vooruit: "forward" — lit. "before-out." Voor (before/in front) + uit (out). Directional prefix.
toekomst: "future" — lit. "to-come." Toe (to/toward) + komst (coming). The future approaches.
Dutch toekomst and English "to come" share Germanic root. The future is what comes toward you.
POLISH (JĘZYK POLSKI) · RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
— POLISH — PASSAGE 1 · CHOPIN / PROVERB —
"Czego się nie robi, tego się nie ma."
What self not does, that self not has.
"What you don't do, you don't have." — Polish proverb. Act to achieve.
— POLISH — PASSAGE 2 · WISŁAWA SZYMBORSKA —
"Każdy początek to tylko ciąg dal…"
"Every beginning is only a continuation…"
— Wisława Szymborska, Nobel Lecture, 1996.
— POLISH — BRIDGE PHRASE · REMEMBER FORWARD —
To zostało stworzone dla ciebie, bezpłatnie, przez ludzi, którzy pamiętali o przyszłości.
This was-created for you, freely, by people who remembered about future.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future."
— LANGUAGE NOTE — "FUTURE" IN POLISH —
przyszłość: "future" — from przyjść (to come/arrive) + abstract suffix. Lit. "that which will arrive."
naprzód: "forward" — directional. Na (on/to) + przód (front). Common in commands: Idź naprzód! (Go forward!).
pamiętali: "they remembered" — from pamiętać (to remember/to keep in mind).
Polish carries memory in its very word for care: pamięć (memory) shares root with pamiętać (to remember).
— DECODING NOTE — FOR FUTURE READERS —
Dutch: ~23M speakers, Netherlands and Belgium. Germanic language closely related to German and English.
Polish: ~45M speakers, Poland + diaspora. West Slavic; closely related to Czech, Slovak, Sorbian.
Geographic link: Amsterdam to Warsaw ~1,100km. Within 500mi radius of Amsterdam, Poland’s western edge.
Both languages use Latin script. Dutch: 26 + ij. Polish: 32 letters including 9 diacritic letters.
See plates 49A–49C for full scripts, phonology, and grammar of Dutch.
PLATE 49D ALT · DUTCH / POLISH · TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
